meaning: A-L-M (three special letters at the start of this chapter)
that (pointing to something far away, feminine)
for those who do good (guidance and mercy for them)
they are completely sure (referring to those who establish prayer and give zakah)
in order to make (someone) go astray
and he takes/adopts it as mockery
upon him (when our verses are recited)
mountains (that Allah placed in the earth)
and so we made (something) grow (after sending down water from the sky)
so show me! (command to a group)
the ones who act unjustly (as subject)
praiseworthy (describing Allah as deserving all praise)
and when (introducing Luqman's advice to his son)
he advises him (Luqman advising his son)
associate partners! (negative command - don't do this)
they both tried with you (referring to parents trying to influence their child)
that (introduces what they are trying to make you do)
in a kind/good manner (describing how to treat parents in this ayah)
whoever (the path of the one who)
Then I will inform you all
with what/whatever (that you used to do)
you all were (referring to what you all used to do)
indeed it (referring to the tiny deed mentioned after)
Most Gentle and Kind (a name of Allah)
and stop! (command to stop something wrong)
afflicted you/befell you (referring to what befalls you in life)
that (used to point to something mentioned)
an arrogant one (described in this ayah as someone Allah does not love)
and lower! (command to one person to lower their voice)
he made someone deny/caused to deny
Have (you) not...? (expecting 'yes, we have' as answer)
they said (responding to what was told to them)
he calls/invites them (referring to those who follow their forefathers instead of Allah's revelation)
then we will inform them emphatically
we let them enjoy greatly (present tense)
they will surely say (with strong emphasis)
Allah (as the object being described in this ayah)
Allah (the one true deity being acted upon)
he causes to enter (Allah is making something enter into something else)
and that (connects to previous statement about Allah controlling day and night)
and that/and indeed (connects to previous statement about Allah being the Truth)
Allah (as the object being referred to)
The Most High (one of Allah's names)
The Most Great (divine attribute of Allah describing His supreme greatness)
so that he shows you all (referring to Allah showing His signs)
indeed, certainly (introduces a statement in this ayah)
then when (connects to previous ayah about waves covering them)
He saved them (referring to when Allah saved the people from the waves)
then from them (referring to those who were saved)
your Lord (addressing all people in ayah)
and all of you fear! (plural command)
a born one (describing someone in this ayah who cannot help their parent)
one who repays (in a possessive relationship)
a self, a soul, a person
All-Aware (as an independent quality of Allah)
